touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Leo Liu at TrueIO. No answer, left voicemail introducing our revenue operations platform and mentioning pipeline visibility challenges common in fintech.

LinkedIn message sent to Leo Liu highlighting pipeline visibility gaps we've solved for similar fintech companies. Leo responded expressing interest in learning more, mentioned he recently joined as Head of RevOps.

questions_asked.question: Do you work with mid-market fintech companies?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have several fintech clients in the 500-1000 employee range with similar RevOps challenges.

touches.ai_summary: Sent detailed email to Leo with case study from similar fintech company, highlighting pipeline visibility and forecasting accuracy improvements. No response yet.
Follow-up email checking if Leo had a chance to review the case study. Leo replied asking about pricing models and implementation timeline, mentioning he's building Q2 budget proposals.

questions_asked.question: What does your pricing look like for a team of around 10-15 users?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing varies based on features and automation tier, but typically $800-1200 per seat annually. Happy to discuss specifics on a call.

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Leo Liu. Discussed TrueIO's current pipeline visibility challenges - using spreadsheets and Salesforce reports that don't give real-time insights. Leo mentioned he's 3 months into the role and tasked with improving forecast accuracy. Team of 12 sellers, looking to scale to 20 by year-end.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need for better pipeline visibility right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our CEO is frustrated with forecast misses. Last quarter we were off by 30%. I need to fix this fast.

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: I'd need to loop in our Implementation Lead Layla Sato for technical review, and probably Madison from our account management team.

questions_asked.question: How quickly could we implement this?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 3-4 weeks for initial setup, then we'd expand automation over time based on your priorities.

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Layla Sato to discuss technical requirements. Layla asked detailed questions about Salesforce integration, data sync frequency, and custom field mapping. She's cautious about implementation complexity given past vendor issues.

questions_asked.question: How does the Salesforce integration work - is it real-time or batch?
questions_asked.answer: Real-time sync via API with 2-minute refresh intervals. You can also trigger manual syncs anytime.

questions_asked.question: We have a lot of custom fields in Salesforce. How flexible is the mapping?
questions_asked.answer: Fully configurable. You can map any standard or custom field during setup.

objections: We've had issues with vendors promising easy integration but requiring heavy customization. Need to see proof this works out of the box.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Leo sharing integration documentation and three fintech customer references. Leo replied thanking us and said he'd review with Layla.

Follow-up email to Leo checking on documentation review. Leo responded saying the references were helpful and he wants to move to discovery phase, but needs to understand pricing better before bringing in more stakeholders.

questions_asked.question: Can you ballpark what 12 seats would cost with the unlimited automation tier?
questions_asked.answer: List pricing would be around $13K annually for 12 seats with unlimited automation. We have some flexibility depending on contract length and use case.

touches.ai_summary: Email to Leo confirming discovery call and sharing agenda focused on pipeline visibility workflow, reporting requirements, and success metrics. Leo confirmed and added that he'd like to discuss automation use cases.
Discovery follow-up call with Leo. Discussed specific automation needs around lead routing and data enrichment. Leo mentioned budget is tight and he'll need to justify ROI clearly to get approval.

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range for this initiative?
questions_asked.answer: I have around $10K allocated, maybe can stretch a bit if the ROI case is strong.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ROI metrics would we see in the first quarter?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see 15-20% forecast accuracy improvement in first 60 days, plus 5-8 hours per week saved in manual reporting.

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Leo. Walked through pipeline dashboard, custom reporting builder, and automation workflows. Leo engaged heavily, asked detailed questions about specific use cases. He's concerned about getting his team trained quickly.

questions_asked.question: How much training does the sales team need to use the dashboard?
questions_asked.answer: Dashboard is intuitive - usually 1 hour training session. Admins need 3-4 hours for automation setup.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the dashboard views per role?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, completely customizable. Reps see their pipeline, managers see team rollups, RevOps sees everything.

questions_asked.question: Which automation features are most important for your Q2 goals?
questions_asked.answer: Lead routing and pipeline alerting are critical. Data enrichment would be nice but secondary.

touches.ai_summary: Second technical evaluation focused on automation workflows. Leo brought up budget constraints more directly, asking about different tiers and what he could drop to hit his $10K budget. Built custom automation scenarios together.

questions_asked.question: If we go with a lower automation tier, can we upgrade later?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. Many customers start with basic automation and expand within 3-6 months.

questions_asked.question: Would you compromise on automation tier to fit budget, or is unlimited automation non-negotiable?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited is really what we need. I'll have to make the case for stretching budget or find savings elsewhere.

objections: The pricing is higher than I expected. I need to figure out how to justify this to finance.
next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with ROI analysis

touches.ai_summary: Email to Leo sharing ROI calculator showing projected forecast accuracy improvement and time savings, translating to $47K annual value. Leo replied saying this is helpful but he needs to get his VP of Sales on board.
next_steps: Schedule meeting with VP of Sales

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Leo (VP of Sales was not able to attend, just Leo). Presented ROI case and full proposal. Leo expressed concern about moving forward without VP buy-in, mentioned he doesn't have budget authority at this level.

questions_asked.question: What would the VP of Sales need to see to approve this?
questions_asked.answer: Proof it works and fits budget. But honestly I'm not sure I can get time on her calendar before end of quarter.

questions_asked.question: Can you share the proposal at list price so I have negotiating room?
questions_asked.answer: I can share the proposal with flexibility noted, but we'd need to understand what budget you're working with.

next_steps: Leo to try to schedule VP meeting

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email checking on VP meeting scheduling. Leo replied saying VP is swamped, but he's been authorized to move forward if he can get it under $9K. He's asking for our best price.

questions_asked.question: What's the lowest you can go for 12 seats with unlimited automation?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my manager. The $13K list is already competitive, but I'll see what flexibility we have.

touches.ai_summary: Email to Leo with updated proposal at $8,800 (32% discount) for 12 seats, unlimited automation, annual contract. Leo replied asking if we can hit $8,300 and throw in extra training hours.

Scheduled exec meeting to finalize terms was rescheduled by Leo due to conflicting finance review. He apologized and asked to move to next week. Sounded slightly stressed about internal approval process.
Email confirming rescheduled meeting. Leo replied briefly confirming, mentioned he's working through internal approvals and needs to finalize by end of month.

Negotiation call with Leo. Discussed final pricing and contract terms. Agreed to $8,300 with standard training package. Leo mentioned he needs to get procurement (Madison Hassan) and Layla to sign off on contract terms.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard payment schedule - can we do quarterly instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Annual upfront is standard at this discount level, but we can discuss quarterly with a smaller discount.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can procurement turn around the contract review?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 3-5 days. I'll flag it as urgent.

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting scheduled to finalize with Madison and Layla was rescheduled again by Leo - Layla had a conflict. Leo sounded apologetic, mentioned things are chaotic with quarter-end.

Final exec meeting with Leo, Madison Hassan, and Layla Sato. Reviewed contract terms, payment schedule (annual upfront), and implementation timeline. Madison asked about data security and compliance. Layla confirmed technical requirements are met. All parties agreed to move forward pending legal review.

questions_asked.question: What compliance certifications do you have - we need SOC 2 and GDPR?
questions_asked.answer: We're SOC 2 Type II certified and GDPR compliant. All documentation is in the security appendix.

questions_asked.question: What's the timeline for legal review?
questions_asked.answer: Madison: Should be 2-3 days, we'll expedite since we're at month-end.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: We can kick off within 5 business days of signed contract. First setup call would be the following week.

next_steps: Target signature by end of week
